hero_description: Master the essential skills of domestic electrical installation and maintenance to build a rewarding career in India's growing construction and real estate sector. This hands-on certification course trains you to safely install, troubleshoot, and maintain electrical systems in residential buildings, preparing you for immediate employment or self-employment opportunities.
overview_description: This foundational course covers electrical theory, safety protocols, wiring systems, circuit installation, fault diagnosis, and maintenance of domestic electrical systems as per Indian Electrical Code (IE Code). Through practical training in real-world scenarios, you'll learn to handle common household electrical tasks, use testing equipment, and follow safety standards. The course is designed for school leavers and working professionals seeking career advancement in the electrical trades sector.

modules.title: Fundamentals of Electricity & Electrical Safety
topics: Basics of electricity — voltage, current, resistance, and power (V, I, R, P concepts explained simply)
AC and DC current — differences and applications in domestic use
Ohm's Law and electrical calculations — practical problems for household circuits
Electrical hazards and shock prevention — understanding earthing and grounding principles
Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) — helmets, gloves, safety goggles, and insulated tools
Fire safety and electrical fires — causes, prevention, and emergency response procedures
Indian Electrical Code (IE Code) compliance — rules for residential installations
First aid for electrical shock — CPR basics and recovery position techniques
Safety signs and symbols — colour codes and warning labels in electrical work
Risk assessment on job sites — identifying hazards before starting work

modules.title: Domestic Wiring Systems & Cable Selection
topics: Types of wiring systems — surface wiring, concealed wiring, and conduit wiring used in Indian homes
Cables and conductors — copper vs aluminium, single core vs multi-core, wire gauges (1.5mm², 2.5mm², 4mm², 6mm²)
Cable colour coding — Indian standard (brown, black, red for phases; blue for neutral; green/yellow for earth)
Insulation materials — PVC, rubber, and their temperature ratings for different areas
Conduits and accessories — PVC pipes, metal conduits, bends, couplings, and junction boxes
Cable sizing calculations — selecting correct wire size based on load and distance
Earthing and grounding systems — soil earthing, pipe earthing, and electrode placement
Switchboards and distribution boards — main switches, MCBs, RCBOs, and their ratings
Cable laying techniques — underground, overhead, and wall-mounted installations
Testing cables before installation — megohm testing and insulation resistance checks

modules.title: Circuit Installation & Wiring Practices
topics: Single-phase circuits for domestic use — 230V supply and circuit design basics
Lighting circuits — series and parallel connections, switch control methods
Power circuits — socket outlets, plug connections, and load distribution
Fan circuits — ceiling fan wiring, regulator connections, and speed control
Water heater and appliance circuits — dedicated circuits for high-load devices
Bell circuits and signalling — doorbell installation and low-voltage signalling systems
Wiring diagrams and schematic drawings — reading and interpreting electrical plans
Installation of switches, sockets, and outlets — proper spacing and height as per standards
Junction box and terminal connections — soldering, crimping, and wire joining methods
Cable routing and management — avoiding interference, proper support, and accessibility
Concealment techniques — hiding cables in walls, conduits, and trunking systems
Labelling and identification — marking circuits and maintaining installation records

modules.title: Testing, Fault Diagnosis & Troubleshooting
topics: Multimeter usage — measuring voltage, current, and resistance in circuits
Insulation testers and megohm meters — checking cable insulation quality
Earth continuity testing — verifying proper earthing connections
Phase identification — using phase testers and neon indicators
Circuit breaker testing — MCB and RCBO functionality checks
Common electrical faults — open circuits, short circuits, earthing faults, and overloads
Fault diagnosis methodology — systematic approach to finding electrical problems
Tracing broken wires — using testers and visual inspection techniques
Switch and socket failures — identifying and replacing faulty components
Lighting issues — bulb failures, ballast problems, and connection faults
Appliance troubleshooting — fans, heaters, pumps, and common household devices
Documentation and reporting — recording faults and maintenance activities

modules.title: Maintenance, Repair & Energy Efficiency
topics: Preventive maintenance schedules — routine checks for residential installations
Cleaning and upkeep — dust removal, corrosion prevention, and terminal maintenance
Replacing switches and sockets — safe removal and installation procedures
Rewiring old installations — upgrading outdated systems safely
Fixing loose connections — tightening terminals and preventing voltage drops
Replacing MCBs and RCBOs — understanding ratings and selecting correct replacements
Repairing damaged cables — splicing, insulation restoration, and temporary repairs
Earthing maintenance — checking soil resistance and electrode conditions
Energy-efficient lighting — LED bulbs, CFL advantages, and power savings
Load balancing — distributing electrical load across phases for efficiency
Identifying energy wastage — phantom loads and unnecessary consumption patterns
Maintenance records and customer communication — documentation and safety advice

modules.title: Professional Skills, Tools & Workplace Ethics
topics: Essential hand tools — screwdrivers, pliers, wire strippers, crimpers, and hammers
Power tools for electricians — drills, impact drivers, and cable cutting equipment
Measuring and marking tools — tape measures, levels, and marking pens
Safety equipment maintenance — checking tool condition and replacing damaged items
Tool care and storage — organizing tools and preventing loss or damage
Customer service skills — communication, professionalism, and handling complaints
Time management and job planning — estimating work duration and scheduling tasks
Workplace safety culture — reporting hazards and following site protocols
Professional ethics — honesty, quality work, and respecting client property
Building a client base — networking, references, and reputation management for self-employed electricians
Cost estimation and pricing — calculating material and labour costs for jobs
Legal compliance — licensing, insurance, and regulatory requirements for electricians in India

assessment.theory_format: 25 Questions × 2 Marks (Multiple Choice and Short Answer)
assessment.practical_format: Hands-on assessment of electrical installation and troubleshooting skills in simulated domestic environment

practical_items.title: Single-Phase Wiring Installation
practical_items.description: Candidate must install a complete lighting and power circuit with proper cable routing, switch connections, socket outlets, and earthing. Work must comply with IE Code standards. Assessed on safety, neatness, and functionality.

practical_items.title: Fault Diagnosis & Repair
practical_items.description: Candidate must identify and repair deliberately introduced electrical faults (broken wire, loose connection, incorrect earthing, short circuit) using appropriate testing equipment and tools. Time limit: 30 minutes.

practical_items.title: Cable Testing & Insulation Check
practical_items.description: Candidate must perform insulation resistance testing, earth continuity testing, and phase identification on prepared cable samples using megohm meter and phase tester. Results must be recorded correctly.

practical_items.title: Tool Safety & Equipment Handling
practical_items.description: Candidate must demonstrate correct use of multimeter, insulation tester, screwdrivers, wire strippers, and crimping tools with proper safety precautions. Assessed on technique and safety awareness.

eligibility: Minimum age: 14 years (or 18 years for some state regulations)
Minimum education: Class 8th pass or equivalent
Already working as electrician? Apply for RPL (Recognition of Prior Learning) exam
Valid photo ID proof required (Aadhaar, PAN, Voter ID, or Driving License)
Medical fitness certificate may be required (check with your state)
Applicable for Indian citizens and registered foreign workers
No upper age limit — career changers welcome
learning_outcomes: Understand electrical fundamentals including voltage, current, resistance, and power calculations
Safely install domestic wiring systems following Indian Electrical Code standards
Select appropriate cables, conduits, and components for residential applications
Design and execute single-phase circuits for lighting, power, and appliances
Diagnose electrical faults using testing equipment and troubleshoot common problems
Perform preventive maintenance and repairs on domestic electrical installations
Apply professional safety practices and workplace ethics in all electrical work
Estimate costs, manage time, and communicate effectively with clients

career_paths.title: Residential Electrician (Employment)
career_paths.description: Work with construction companies, real estate developers, or electrical contractors installing and maintaining wiring in residential buildings. Salary increases with experience and certifications.

career_paths.title: Self-Employed Electrician
career_paths.description: Start your own electrical business serving household clients. Income depends on job volume, reputation, and service quality. Many electricians earn significantly more through direct client relationships.

career_paths.title: Maintenance Electrician (Residential Complexes)
career_paths.description: Work in apartment complexes, housing societies, or residential colonies handling preventive maintenance and repairs. Offers stable income with regular working hours.

career_paths.title: Electrical Supervisor/Foreman
career_paths.description: After gaining 2-3 years experience, supervise teams of electricians on construction sites. Requires additional skills in team management and project coordination.

career_paths.title: Electrical Contractor
career_paths.description: Establish a contracting business bidding for residential projects, managing teams, and handling multiple installations. Requires business registration and insurance.

career_paths.title: Electrician Trainer/Instructor
career_paths.description: After 5+ years experience, teach electrical skills at ITIs, training centers, or private institutes. Requires communication skills and passion for knowledge sharing.

additional_information.course_structure: This 12-week course combines classroom instruction (60 hours) with practical hands-on training (120 hours) in fully equipped electrical labs with simulated domestic installations.
additional_information.training_environment: Candidates train in realistic domestic electrical setups including switchboards, lighting circuits, power circuits, and fault simulation boards. All equipment meets Indian safety standards.
additional_information.industry_relevance: Content aligned with National Occupational Standards (NOS) for Domestic Electrician and Indian Electrical Code (IE Code) requirements. Covers real-world scenarios faced by electricians in Indian residential sector.
additional_information.certification_validity: CETCF certification valid across India for 3 years. Renewable through refresher courses. Recognized by state electricity boards and construction industry bodies.
additional_information.further_progression: Graduates can pursue Advanced Electrician Certification, Electrical Supervisor course, or specialized training in solar installations, smart home systems, or industrial electrical work.
additional_information.placement_support: Training centers provide job placement assistance, industry connections, and help in building client base for self-employment. Many successful graduates earn ₹50,000-₹1,00,000+ monthly through established practices.
additional_information.government_recognition: Course follows Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY) guidelines. Eligible candidates may receive government training subsidies. Certification helps in obtaining state electrical licenses.
additional_information.tools_provided: Training centers provide all essential tools and equipment. Candidates can purchase personal toolkit (₹5,000-₹10,000) for professional practice after certification.
additional_information.language_medium: Instruction available in Hindi, English, and regional languages. All technical terms explained in simple, accessible language with visual demonstrations.
additional_information.success_rate: Approximately 85% of trained candidates pass certification exam. High employment rate within 3 months of certification in urban and semi-urban areas.
